[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879年}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year }}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year }}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year }}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year }}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.text:
[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year }} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year }}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year }}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year }}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year }}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|12=draft|12=draft}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|12=draft|12=draft}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|12=draft|12=draft}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|12=draft|12=draft}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|12=draft|12=draft}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|12=draft|12=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=draft|12=draft}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|12=draft|12=draft}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=draft|12=draft}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=draft|12=draft}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|12=draft|12=draft}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=draft|12=draft}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|12=draft|12=draft}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=draft|12=draft}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|12=draft|12=draft}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|12=draft|12=draft}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|12=draft|12=draft}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=draft|12=draft}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=draft|12=draft}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|12=draft|12=draft}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=draft|12=draft}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=draft|12=draft}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=draft|12=draft}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).

Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. revision.comment: 73 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:真言宗善通寺派 up to rev 83136652
revision.text:
[[ファイル:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|真言宗善通寺派宗務庁（[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]）]] '''真言宗善通寺派'''（しんごんしゅうぜんつうじは）は、[[日本]]における真言系[[仏教]]宗派のひとつで、[[真言宗#古義派|古義真言宗]]に属する。'''総本山は[[善通寺]]。''' *宗祖 弘法大師（[[空海]]） *派祖 [[増俊]]僧正
== 法流 == * 随心院流
== 宗紋 == *十六菊複瓣（ふくへん） *心に善字
== 寺格（順不同） == [[ファイル:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|総本山 善通寺]] * '''総本山''' [[善通寺]]（[[香川県]][[善通寺市]][[善通寺町]]）
* '''大本山''' **[[随心院]]（[[京都市]][[山科区]]小野御霊町） **[[弥谷寺]]（[[香川県]][[三豊市]]） **[[吉祥寺 (佐賀県基山町)|吉祥寺]]（[[佐賀県]][[基山町]]）
* '''別格本山''' **浄光寺（[[兵庫県]][[尼崎市]]） **[[祇園寺 (高梁市)|祇園寺]]（[[岡山県]][[高梁市]]） **[[童学寺]]（[[徳島県]][[石井町]]） **[[與田寺]]（[[香川県]][[東かがわ市]]） **実相寺（香川県[[さぬき市]]） **西方寺（香川県さぬき市） **[[与田寺]]（香川県[[東かがわ市]]） **観智院（香川県[[善通寺市]]） **[[曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市) |曼荼羅寺]]（香川県善通寺市）
* '''その他の末寺''' **[[福勝寺]]（京都市上京区） **[[志度寺]]（香川県さぬき市） **[[大興寺 (三豊市)|大興寺]]（香川県[[三豊市]]） **善通寺関東別院 (神奈川県座間市)
==沿革== 真言宗善通寺派の歴史は、[[仁海]]が曼荼羅寺（後の[[随心院]]）を開創したことに始まる。仁海は[[真言宗]]の[[真言宗#事相と教相|事相]]（真言密教の儀礼・作法）の奥義を極めた。その名声を慕って多くの入門者が集まり、門下から[[成尊]]などの多くの名僧を輩出した。仁海が伝えた事相は、[[真言宗#小野流|小野流]]と称され、仁海が流祖とされた。
[[1115年]]（[[永久 (元号)|永久]]3年）増俊が[[勧修寺]]勝福院において[[厳覚]]から[[伝法灌頂]]を受けて、その法燈を継いだ。仁海創建の曼荼羅寺の子院であった増俊の住坊であった随心院において、増俊が事相の流派である「随心院流」を分派した。このころから、曼荼羅寺は随心院と称されるようになった。
[[明治政府]]の宗教政策により、他の真言宗宗派と[[1879年]]（明治12年）に合同する。多くの寺院が離脱していくなかで[[東寺]]を中心した四派聯合を形成していたものの、[[1907年]]（明治40年）、随心院を本山とする'''真言宗小野派'''として独立する。
[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）3月14日には、真言宗小野派の宗規を改正して'''真言宗善通寺派'''と改称した。このときに随心院に[[能化]]職を置き、宗務所を善通寺に設けることになった。
[[1941年]]（昭和16年）3月、古義真言宗・新義真言宗系の宗派が政府の政策によって合同し、大真言宗が成立する。
戦後、大真言宗から独立し、[[1947年]]（昭和22年）4月、単称の真言宗と公称したが、他の真言宗各派の均衡を慮って[[1972年]]（昭和47年）7月17日に真言宗善通寺派と称して現在に至っている。
==善通寺歴代（真言宗善通寺派管長）== (東寺長者兼務） # [[実恵]] # [[真済]] # [[真雅]] # [[宗叡]] # [[真然]] # [[益信]] # [[聖宝]] # [[観賢]] # 延敒 # 観宿 # 済高 # [[貞崇]] # 泰舜 # [[寛空]] # 救世 # 寛静 # [[定照]] # [[寛朝]] # [[雅慶]] # 深覚 # [[済信]] # 深覚 # [[仁海]] # 深覚 # 仁海 # 深観 # [[覚源]] # [[長信]] # 成尊 # 良深 # 信覚 # [[定賢]] # 頼観 # 経範 # [[範俊]] # [[寛助]] # [[勝覚]] # 信証 # [[定海]] # [[定海]] # [[寛信]] # [[寛遍]] # 寛信 # 寛遍 # 禎喜 # 定遍 # [[俊証]] # [[覚成]] # [[延杲]] # 印性 # [[道尊 (以仁王王子)|道尊]] # 成宝 # 道尊
（[[随心院]]門跡兼務） # [[親厳]] # 厳海 # 宣厳 # 俊厳 # 厳恵 # 静厳 # 厳家
（[[大覚寺]]門跡兼務） # [[後宇多天皇|後宇多法皇]] # [[性円法親王]]（[[後宇多天皇]]皇子） # 寛尊法親王（[[亀山天皇]]皇子） # 性勝法親王（[[後宇多天皇]]皇子） # [[恒性法親王]]（[[後醍醐天皇]]皇子） # 深守法親王（木寺宮[[邦良親王]]子）
（随心院門跡～明治維新） # 経厳 # 厳海 # 宣厳 # 俊厳 # 厳恵 # [[静厳]] # 厳家 # 経厳 # 通厳 # 照厳 # 厳叡 # 祐厳 # 厳宝 # 持厳 # 忠厳 # 仙朝 # 長静 # 増孝 # 栄厳 # 俊海 # [[堯厳]] # 増護
(中興:誕生院住持) # 宥範 # 宥源 # 宥快 # 宥栄 # 宥紹 # 宥鎮 # 宥深 # 宥鋭 # 宥應 # 宥助 # 宥筌 # 宥成 # 尊翁 # 夤瓊 # 夤貞 # 宥謙 # 光胤 # 円龍 # 光歓 # 光天 # 光国 # 寛充 # 厳蔵 # 厳曉 # 慈定 # 厳猷 # 慈定
（明治維新、善通寺および誕生院統一） # 佐伯旭雅 # 佐伯法遵 # 松井宥粲 # 蓮生観善(1) # 亀谷宥英(2) # 蓮生善隆(3) # 高吉清順(4) # 樫原禅澄(5) # 菅智潤(6) *( )内は、善通寺派管長歴代。
== 年中行事== *1月1日 修正会 *1月1～3日 特別[[護摩]]祈祷 *2月 3日 [[節分]]会・[[星供]]結願修行 *2月第4週（土・日） 大会陽（はだかまつり） *4月8日 仏生会（[[灌仏会|花まつり]]） *4月第3週（土日） 正御影供 *5月 [[ゴールデンウイーク]] [[五重塔]]内部特別公開 *6月 14～15日 弘法大師誕生会（青葉まつり） *6月中旬 お大師市（善通寺境内） *7月 16日～8月16日 舎利講 *7月第4土曜 萬燈萬華会 *7月（[[土用]]丑の日） [[キュウリ|胡瓜]]加持 *8月14～15日 [[盂蘭盆会]] *10月20～21日 永代土砂加持 *11月3日 佐伯祖廟御法楽（空海まつり） *12月 [[冬至]] 星供開白 ===毎月行事=== *毎朝 朝勤行（御影堂） **5月1日～9月30日 午前5時30分～ **10月1日～4月30日 午前6時～ *8日 [[薬師如来]]御法楽 *15日 [[釈迦如来]]御法楽 *15日 弘法大師誕生会 *15日 [[パゴダ]]供養 *毎月20日 月例永代経 *21日 御影供 *21日 [[写経]]会（法話） *28日 護摩供 *第3[[水曜日]] [[仏画]]教室・第4[[日曜日]] 仏画教室
== 宗務組織 == *'''法主（管長）'''（任期5年・選挙制） *'''宗務庁'''（善通寺内） *'''内局''' **宗務総長 **執行長（執行を統括） **庶務部 **教学部 **財務部 （庶務部・教学部・財務部に部長1名を置く） *'''宗会'''（地方教区長が議員として選出・任期4年） **議長・副議長を置く **定期宗会（年1回）・臨時宗会 *'''地方宗務組織''' **日本国内に11教区を置く（各教区に教区長・副教区長を置く）
== 関連組織 == *善通寺教学振興会 *善通寺遍照講本部 *善通寺遍照会
==僧階・僧籍== *僧階 全15級（15級の僧階の他に教師試補を置く） **1級 [[大僧正]] **2級 権大僧正 **3級 中僧正 **4級 権中僧正 **5級 少僧正 **6級 権小僧正 **7級 大僧都 **8級 権大僧都 **9級 中僧都 **10級 権中僧都 **11級 少僧都 **12級 権小僧都 **13級 大律師 **14級 律師 **15級 権律師 *権大僧都（真言宗各派経営の大学卒業者） *中僧都（真言宗各派経営以外の大学卒業者で検定合格者） *住職（真言宗善通寺派教師。[[得度]]と[[度牒]]を受けた後、[[四度加行]]の後、[[伝法灌頂]]に入壇。真言宗善通寺派僧籍簿に登録した者）
== 布教活動 == *本部布教（巡教） *地方布教 *特殊布教 **文書・映像 **外国布教 *出版物 **宗報
== 教育機関 == *[[善通寺専修学院]]（善通寺内） *[[随流講傳所]]（随心院内） *[[洛南高等学校・附属中学校]]（協同経営） *[[種智院大学]]（協同経営）
== 施設 == *[[宿坊]]・いろは会館（善通寺内に設置） **大師の里湯[[温泉]] *宝物館（善通寺内に設置） *讃岐学園（児童養護施設）（[[香川県]][[高松市]]）
==教義== [[古義真言宗]]の教義に準じる。

revision.text:
[[ファイル:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|真言宗善通寺派宗務庁（[[香川県]][[善通寺市]]）]] '''真言宗善通寺派'''（しんごんしゅうぜんつうじは）は、[[日本]]における真言系[[仏教]]宗派のひとつで、[[真言宗#古義派|古義真言宗]]に属する。'''総本山は[[善通寺]]。''' *宗祖 弘法大師（[[空海]]） *派祖 [[増俊]]僧正
== 法流 == * 随心院流
== 宗紋 == *十六菊複瓣（ふくへん） *心に善字
== 寺格（順不同） == [[ファイル:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|総本山 善通寺]] * '''総本山''' [[善通寺]]（[[香川県]][[善通寺市]][[善通寺町]]）
* '''大本山''' **[[随心院]]（[[京都市]][[山科区]]小野御霊町） **[[弥谷寺]]（[[香川県]][[三豊市]]） **[[吉祥寺 (佐賀県基山町)|吉祥寺]]（[[佐賀県]][[基山町]]）
* '''別格本山''' **浄光寺（[[兵庫県]][[尼崎市]]） **[[祇園寺 (高梁市)|祇園寺]]（[[岡山県]][[高梁市]]） **[[童学寺]]（[[徳島県]][[石井町]]） **[[與田寺]]（[[香川県]][[東かがわ市]]） **実相寺（香川県[[さぬき市]]） **西方寺（香川県さぬき市） **[[与田寺]]（香川県[[東かがわ市]]） **観智院（香川県[[善通寺市]]） **[[曼荼羅寺 (善通寺市) |曼荼羅寺]]（香川県善通寺市）
* '''その他の末寺''' **[[福勝寺]]（京都市上京区） **[[志度寺]]（香川県さぬき市） **[[大興寺 (三豊市)|大興寺]]（香川県[[三豊市]]） **善通寺関東別院 (神奈川県座間市)
==沿革== 真言宗善通寺派の歴史は、[[仁海]]が曼荼羅寺（後の[[随心院]]）を開創したことに始まる。仁海は[[真言宗]]の[[真言宗#事相と教相|事相]]（真言密教の儀礼・作法）の奥義を極めた。その名声を慕って多くの入門者が集まり、門下から[[成尊]]などの多くの名僧を輩出した。仁海が伝えた事相は、[[真言宗#小野流|小野流]]と称され、仁海が流祖とされた。
[[1115年]]（[[永久 (元号)|永久]]3年）増俊が[[勧修寺]]勝福院において[[厳覚]]から[[伝法灌頂]]を受けて、その法燈を継いだ。仁海創建の曼荼羅寺の子院であった増俊の住坊であった随心院において、増俊が事相の流派である「随心院流」を分派した。このころから、曼荼羅寺は随心院と称されるようになった。
[[明治政府]]の宗教政策により、他の真言宗宗派と[[1879年]]（明治12年）に合同する。多くの寺院が離脱していくなかで[[東寺]]を中心した四派聯合を形成していたものの、[[1907年]]（明治40年）、随心院を本山とする'''真言宗小野派'''として独立する。
[[1931年]]（[[昭和]]6年）3月14日には、真言宗小野派の宗規を改正して'''真言宗善通寺派'''と改称した。このときに随心院に[[能化]]職を置き、宗務所を善通寺に設けることになった。
[[1941年]]（昭和16年）3月、古義真言宗・新義真言宗系の宗派が政府の政策によって合同し、大真言宗が成立する。
戦後、大真言宗から独立し、[[1947年]]（昭和22年）4月、単称の真言宗と公称したが、他の真言宗各派の均衡を慮って[[1972年]]（昭和47年）7月17日に真言宗善通寺派と称して現在に至っている。
==善通寺歴代（真言宗善通寺派管長）== (東寺長者兼務） # [[実恵]] # [[真済]] # [[真雅]] # [[宗叡]] # [[真然]] # [[益信]] # [[聖宝]] # [[観賢]] # 延敒 # 観宿 # 済高 # [[貞崇]] # 泰舜 # [[寛空]] # 救世 # 寛静 # [[定照]] # [[寛朝]] # [[雅慶]] # 深覚 # [[済信]] # 深覚 # [[仁海]] # 深覚 # 仁海 # 深観 # [[覚源]] # [[長信]] # 成尊 # 良深 # 信覚 # [[定賢]] # 頼観 # 経範 # [[範俊]] # [[寛助]] # [[勝覚]] # 信証 # [[定海]] # [[定海]] # [[寛信]] # [[寛遍]] # 寛信 # 寛遍 # 禎喜 # 定遍 # [[俊証]] # [[覚成]] # [[延杲]] # 印性 # [[道尊 (以仁王王子)|道尊]] # 成宝 # 道尊
（[[随心院]]門跡兼務） # [[親厳]] # 厳海 # 宣厳 # 俊厳 # 厳恵 # 静厳 # 厳家
（[[大覚寺]]門跡兼務） # [[後宇多天皇|後宇多法皇]] # [[性円法親王]]（[[後宇多天皇]]皇子） # 寛尊法親王（[[亀山天皇]]皇子） # 性勝法親王（[[後宇多天皇]]皇子） # [[恒性法親王]]（[[後醍醐天皇]]皇子） # 深守法親王（木寺宮[[邦良親王]]子）
（随心院門跡～明治維新） # 経厳 # 厳海 # 宣厳 # 俊厳 # 厳恵 # [[静厳]] # 厳家 # 経厳 # 通厳 # 照厳 # 厳叡 # 祐厳 # 厳宝 # 持厳 # 忠厳 # 仙朝 # 長静 # 増孝 # 栄厳 # 俊海 # [[堯厳]] # 増護
(中興:誕生院住持) # 宥範 # 宥源 # 宥快 # 宥栄 # 宥紹 # 宥鎮 # 宥深 # 宥鋭 # 宥應 # 宥助 # 宥筌 # 宥成 # 尊翁 # 夤瓊 # 夤貞 # 宥謙 # 光胤 # 円龍 # 光歓 # 光天 # 光国 # 寛充 # 厳蔵 # 厳曉 # 慈定 # 厳猷 # 慈定
（明治維新、善通寺および誕生院統一） # 佐伯旭雅 # 佐伯法遵 # 松井宥粲 # 蓮生観善(1) # 亀谷宥英(2) # 蓮生善隆(3) # 高吉清順(4) # 樫原禅澄(5) # 菅智潤(6) *( )内は、善通寺派管長歴代。
== 年中行事== *1月1日 修正会 *1月1～3日 特別[[護摩]]祈祷 *2月 3日 [[節分]]会・[[星供]]結願修行 *2月第4週（土・日） 大会陽（はだかまつり） *4月8日 仏生会（[[灌仏会|花まつり]]） *4月第3週（土日） 正御影供 *5月 [[ゴールデンウイーク]] [[五重塔]]内部特別公開 *6月 14～15日 弘法大師誕生会（青葉まつり） *6月中旬 お大師市（善通寺境内） *7月 16日～8月16日 舎利講 *7月第4土曜 萬燈萬華会 *7月（[[土用]]丑の日） [[キュウリ|胡瓜]]加持 *8月14～15日 [[盂蘭盆会]] *10月20～21日 永代土砂加持 *11月3日 佐伯祖廟御法楽（空海まつり） *12月 [[冬至]] 星供開白 ===毎月行事=== *毎朝 朝勤行（御影堂） **5月1日～9月30日 午前5時30分～ **10月1日～4月30日 午前6時～ *8日 [[薬師如来]]御法楽 *15日 [[釈迦如来]]御法楽 *15日 弘法大師誕生会 *15日 [[パゴダ]]供養 *毎月20日 月例永代経 *21日 御影供 *21日 [[写経]]会（法話） *28日 護摩供 *第3[[水曜日]] [[仏画]]教室・第4[[日曜日]] 仏画教室
== 宗務組織 == *'''法主（管長）'''（任期5年・選挙制） *'''宗務庁'''（善通寺内） *'''内局''' **宗務総長 **執行長（執行を統括） **庶務部 **教学部 **財務部 （庶務部・教学部・財務部に部長1名を置く） *'''宗会'''（地方教区長が議員として選出・任期4年） **議長・副議長を置く **定期宗会（年1回）・臨時宗会 *'''地方宗務組織''' **日本国内に11教区を置く（各教区に教区長・副教区長を置く）
== 関連組織 == *善通寺教学振興会 *善通寺遍照講本部 *善通寺遍照会
==僧階・僧籍== *僧階 全15級（15級の僧階の他に教師試補を置く） **1級 [[大僧正]] **2級 権大僧正 **3級 中僧正 **4級 権中僧正 **5級 少僧正 **6級 権小僧正 **7級 大僧都 **8級 権大僧都 **9級 中僧都 **10級 権中僧都 **11級 少僧都 **12級 権小僧都 **13級 大律師 **14級 律師 **15級 権律師 *権大僧都（真言宗各派経営の大学卒業者） *中僧都（真言宗各派経営以外の大学卒業者で検定合格者） *住職（真言宗善通寺派教師。[[得度]]と[[度牒]]を受けた後、[[四度加行]]の後、[[伝法灌頂]]に入壇。真言宗善通寺派僧籍簿に登録した者）
== 布教活動 == *本部布教（巡教） *地方布教 *特殊布教 **文書・映像 **外国布教 *出版物 **宗報
== 教育機関 == *[[善通寺専修学院]]（善通寺内） *[[随流講傳所]]（随心院内） *[[洛南高等学校・附属中学校]]（協同経営） *[[種智院大学]]（協同経営）
== 施設 == *[[宿坊]]・いろは会館（善通寺内に設置） **大師の里湯[[温泉]] *宝物館（善通寺内に設置） *讃岐学園（児童養護施設）（[[香川県]][[高松市]]）
==教義== [[古義真言宗]]の教義に準じる。

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

}}

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.

== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Zentsuji Town).

* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Hyogo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).

Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.

Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|qq=}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1931|qq=}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1972|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.text:
[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashina-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoyo|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefecture|qq=}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikagawa|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zuishin-in|qq=}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1115|qq=}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Denbō Kanjo|qq=}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshuji|qq=}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|qq=}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1931|qq=}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1972|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
