== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashina-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoyo|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefecture|qq=}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikagawa|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zuishin-in|qq=}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1115|qq=}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Denbō Kanjo|qq=}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshuji|qq=}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|qq=}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1931|qq=}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1972|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.

== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.

* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikagawa|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).

The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.

Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972nd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1972|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[sn:Shingon Zentsuji Sect]] [[ja:真言宗善通寺派]] [[en:Shingon Zentsuji Sect]]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|ja|空海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kūkai|qq=}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsuji|ja|善通寺市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zentsuji|qq=}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|ja|香川県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagawa Prefecture|qq=}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ({{ill|Yamashina-ku|ja|山科区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashina-ku|qq=}}, {{ill|Kyoto|ja|京都市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyoto|qq=}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo|ja|三豊市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mitoyo|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama|ja|基山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiyama|qq=}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|ja|佐賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saga Prefecture|qq=}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|ja|尼崎市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amagasaki|qq=}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|ja|兵庫県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hyōgo Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi|ja|高梁市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takahashi|qq=}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|ja|岡山県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okayama Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Prefecture|qq=}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa|ja|東かがわ市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashikagawa|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki|ja|さぬき市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki|qq=}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|ja|随心院|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zuishin-in|qq=}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|ja|1115年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1115|qq=}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the {{ill|Denbō Kanjo|ja|伝法灌頂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Denbō Kanjo|qq=}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at {{ill|Kanshuji|ja|勧修寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshuji|qq=}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|ja|1879年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1879|qq=}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tōji|ja|東寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōji|qq=}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|ja|1907年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1907|qq=}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|ja|1931st year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1931|qq=}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/World War II|qq=}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|ja|1972年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1972|qq=}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|ja|節分|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun|qq=}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).

revision.comment: Bot: update category → Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha
revision.text:
[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).

Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.

Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].

Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[Japanese Buddhism]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.text:
[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[Meiji government]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.text:
[[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
