== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|qq=|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|qq=|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|qq=|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|qq=|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

{{wikidata link|Q11583514}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Schools of Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha]] [[Category:Traditional Buddhist Sects]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Pages with conflicting qids]] to [[Category:Qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]].]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhism in Japan]], belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: [[:en:Kūkai|Kūkai]] (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ([[:en:Zentsūji, Kagawa|Zentsuji]], [[:en:Kagawa Prefecture|Kagawa Prefecture]], Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]] ([[:en:Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|Yamashina-ku]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto]], Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ([[:en:Mitoyo, Kagawa|Mitoyo]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ([[:en:Kiyama, Saga|Kiyama]], [[:en:Saga Prefecture|Saga Prefecture]]).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ([[:en:Amagasaki|Amagasaki]], [[:en:Hyōgo Prefecture|Hyōgo Prefecture]]). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ([[:en:Takahashi, Okayama|Takahashi]], [[:en:Okayama Prefecture|Okayama Prefecture]]). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ([[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]). ** [[Yodaji]] ([[:en:Higashikagawa, Kagawa|Higashikagawa]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ([[:en:Sanuki, Kagawa|Sanuki]], Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later [[:en:Zuishin-in|Zuishin-in]]). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In [[:en:1115|1115]] (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the [[:en:Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|Denbō Kanjo]] from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at [[:en:Kajū-ji|Kanshuji]] Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the [[:en:Government of Meiji Japan|Government of Meiji Japan]]'s religious policy in [[:en:1879|1879]], Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on [[:en:Tō-ji|Tōji]], they faced secessions. In [[:en:1907|1907]], the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In [[:en:1931|1931]], the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During [[:en:World War II|World War II]], Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in [[:en:1972|1972]].
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special [[:en:goma|goma]] prayers. * **February 3**: [[:en:Setsubun|Setsubun]] ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * [[:en:Shuchiin University|Shuchiin University]] (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]
{{translated page|ja|真言宗善通寺派|version=83136652|comment=Imported full ja history}}

During {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma}} prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University}} (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{wikidata link|Q11583514}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Schools of Shingon Buddhism]] [[Category:Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha]] [[Category:Traditional Buddhist Sects]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:真言宗善通寺派]][[zh:真言宗善通寺派]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11583514]] Wikidata name: Shingon Sect Zentsuji School [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} [[File:Zentsuji-ha office Zentsuji01n4592.jpg|thumb|300px|The administrative office of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha in [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]], {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}.]] '''Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha''' (真言宗善通寺派, Shingon-shū Zentsūji-ha) is one of the Shingon sects of {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhism in Japan}}, belonging to the [[Kogi Shingon-shū|Kogi (Old) Shingon Buddhism]]. The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].
* Founder: {{ill|Kūkai|en|Kūkai|WD=Q222227|lt=Kūkai}} (posthumously known as Kōbō Daishi). * Sect Founder: {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
== Lineage == * Zuishin-in lineage.
== Sect Crest == * Sixteen-petaled chrysanthemum. * The character 善 ("Zen") within a heart shape.
== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** {{ill|Zuishin-in|en|Zuishin-in|WD=Q11658945|lt=Zuishin-in}} ({{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo, Kagawa|en|Mitoyo, Kagawa|WD=Q873578|lt=Mitoyo}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama, Saga|en|Kiyama, Saga|WD=Q1206602|lt=Kiyama}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|WD=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi, Okayama|en|Takahashi, Okayama|WD=Q851675|lt=Takahashi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|en|Ishii, Tokushima|WD=Q1205943|lt=Ishii}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|en|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|WD=Q861255|lt=Higashikagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|en|Zuishin-in|WD=Q11658945|lt=Zuishin-in}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|en|1115|WD=Q19534|lt=1115}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the {{ill|Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|en|Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|WD=Q108696143|lt=Denbō Kanjo}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Kajū-ji|en|Kajū-ji|WD=Q772598|lt=Kanshuji}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}}'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tō-ji|en|Tō-ji|WD=Q1046403|lt=Tōji}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|en|1931|WD=Q18782|lt=1931}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma}} prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University}} (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|WD=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi, Okayama|en|Takahashi, Okayama|WD=Q851675|lt=Takahashi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|en|Ishii, Tokushima|WD=Q1205943|lt=Ishii}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|en|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|WD=Q861255|lt=Higashikagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).

== Temple Rankings == [[File:Zentsu-ji in Zentsu-ji City Kagawa pref16s5s4020.jpg|thumb|Zentsuji, the head temple.]]
* '''Head Temple:''' ** [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]] ({{ill|Zentsūji, Kagawa|en|Zentsūji, Kagawa|WD=Q191300|lt=Zentsuji}}, {{ill|Kagawa Prefecture|en|Kagawa Prefecture|WD=Q161454|lt=Kagawa Prefecture}}, Zentsuji Town).
* '''Major Head Temples:''' ** {{ill|Zuishin-in|en|Zuishin-in|WD=Q11658945|lt=Zuishin-in}} ({{ill|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|en|Yamashina-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1076993|lt=Yamashina-ku}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto}}, Ono Goryo Town). ** [[Iyayaji]] ({{ill|Mitoyo, Kagawa|en|Mitoyo, Kagawa|WD=Q873578|lt=Mitoyo}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Kisshoji (Saga)|Kisshoji]] ({{ill|Kiyama, Saga|en|Kiyama, Saga|WD=Q1206602|lt=Kiyama}}, {{ill|Saga Prefecture|en|Saga Prefecture|WD=Q160420|lt=Saga Prefecture}}).
* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|WD=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi, Okayama|en|Takahashi, Okayama|WD=Q851675|lt=Takahashi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|en|Ishii, Tokushima|WD=Q1205943|lt=Ishii}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|en|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|WD=Q861255|lt=Higashikagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).
== History == The origins of Shingon Buddhism Zentsuji-ha trace back to {{ill|Ninkai|ja|仁海|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninkai|1=Ningai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11376417}}, who founded Mandaraji (later {{ill|Zuishin-in|en|Zuishin-in|WD=Q11658945|lt=Zuishin-in}}). Renowned for mastering Shingon [[:en:Rituals|真言宗#事相と教相]], his teachings attracted many disciples, producing notable monks such as {{ill|Jōson|ja|成尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōson|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Ninkai's teachings were named the {{ill|Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|真言宗#小野流|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ono lineage (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Shingon Buddhism|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q550182}}, establishing him as the founder of this stream.
In {{ill|1115|en|1115|WD=Q19534|lt=1115}} (Eikyū 3), {{ill|Zoshun|ja|増俊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zoshun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} received the {{ill|Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|en|Abhiṣeka (Buddhism)|WD=Q108696143|lt=Denbō Kanjo}} from {{ill|Gonkaku|ja|厳覚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gonkaku|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} at {{ill|Kajū-ji|en|Kajū-ji|WD=Q772598|lt=Kanshuji}} Shōfuku-in, inheriting the Dharma lineage. Zoshun developed the "Zuishin-in lineage" of rituals at Zuishin-in, which had evolved from Ninkai's Mandaraji.
Under the {{ill|Government of Meiji Japan|en|Government of Meiji Japan|WD=Q1322181|lt=Government of Meiji Japan}}'s religious policy in {{ill|1879|en|1879|WD=Q7783|lt=1879}}, Zentsuji-ha temporarily merged with other Shingon sects. Despite forming alliances centered on {{ill|Tō-ji|en|Tō-ji|WD=Q1046403|lt=Tōji}}, they faced secessions. In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|en|1931|WD=Q18782|lt=1931}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma}} prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=hoshimatsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
== Educational Institutions == * [[Zentsuji Training Academy]]. * [[Zuishin-in Seminar]]. * [[Rakunan High School and Junior High School]] (cooperatively managed). * {{ill|Shuchiin University|en|Shuchiin University|WD=Q7504609|lt=Shuchiin University}} (cooperatively managed).
== External Links == * [http://www.zentsuji.com/ Head Temple Zentsuji] * [http://www.zuishinin.or.jp/ Zuishin-in] * [http://www.shuchiin.ac.jp/ Shuchiin University] * [http://www.rakunan-h.ed.jp/ Rakunan High School and Junior High School]

The '''head temple''' is [[:en:Zentsuji|Zentsuji]].

* '''Special Head Temples:''' ** Jokōji ({{ill|Amagasaki|en|Amagasaki|WD=Q213318|lt=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|Hyōgo Prefecture|en|Hyōgo Prefecture|WD=Q130290|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture}}). ** [[Gionji (Takahashi)|Gionji]] ({{ill|Takahashi, Okayama|en|Takahashi, Okayama|WD=Q851675|lt=Takahashi}}, {{ill|Okayama Prefecture|en|Okayama Prefecture|WD=Q132936|lt=Okayama Prefecture}}). ** [[Dōgakuji]] ({{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|en|Ishii, Tokushima|WD=Q1205943|lt=Ishii}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}). ** [[Yodaji]] ({{ill|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|en|Higashikagawa, Kagawa|WD=Q861255|lt=Higashikagawa}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Jissōji ({{ill|Sanuki, Kagawa|en|Sanuki, Kagawa|WD=Q857202|lt=Sanuki}}, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Saihōji (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Yodaji (Higashikagawa, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Kanchi-in (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Mandaraji (Zentsuji)|Mandaraji]] (Zentsuji, Kagawa Prefecture).
* '''Other Subordinate Temples:''' ** [[Fukushoji]] (Kamigyo-ku, Kyoto). ** [[Shidoji]] (Sanuki, Kagawa Prefecture). ** [[Daikōji (Mitoyo)|Daikōji]] (Mitoyo, Kagawa Prefecture). ** Zentsuji Kanto Betsuin (Zama, Kanagawa Prefecture).

In {{ill|1907|en|1907|WD=Q2048|lt=1907|1=1907}}, the group became independent as '''Shingon-shū Ono-ha''', with Zuishin-in as its head temple.
In {{ill|1931|en|1931|WD=Q18782|lt=1931|1=1931}}, the sect's regulations were revised, renaming it '''Shingon-shū Zentsuji-ha'''. The main administrative office moved to Zentsuji, and the position of {{ill|Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|ja|能化|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Noka (Shingon Buddhism)|1=Q11611944|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11611944}} was established at Zuishin-in.
During {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=World War II|1=World War II}}, Shingon schools merged into the government-backed {{ill|Dai Shingon-shū|ja|大真言宗|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dai Shingon-shū|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Post-war, the sect regained independence, adopting its current name in {{ill|1972|en|1972|WD=Q2476|lt=1972|1=1972}}.
== Annual Events == * **January 1–3**: Special {{ill|goma|en|goma|WD=Q204381|lt=goma|1=Goma}} prayers. * **February 3**: {{ill|Setsubun|en|Setsubun|WD=Q746798|lt=Setsubun|1=Setsubun}} ceremonies. * **February (4th week)**: Naked Festival (Daikōyo). * **April 8**: Buddha's Birthday. * **April (3rd week)**: Grand Mieiku ceremony. * **May (Golden Week)**: Special opening of the {{ill|Five-storied Pagoda|ja|五重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Five-storied Pagoda|1=Five-storied pagoda|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11372713}}. * **June 14–15**: Kōbō Daishi's Birth Festival (Aoba Festival). * **July (土用丑の日)**: Cucumber blessing. * **August 14–15**: {{ill|Obon|ja|盂蘭盆会|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Obon|1=Ullambana Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q15898039}}. * **November 3**: Sabaekusobyo Hōraku (Kūkai Festival). * **December**: Winter solstice {{ill|hoshiku|ja|星供|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hoshiku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 星まつり|1=Star Festival|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512767}}.
== Organization == * **Head:** Dharma Lord (elected every 5 years). * **Administrative Office:** Located in Zentsuji. * **Departments:** General Affairs, Teaching, Finance. * **Districts:** 11 administrative regions in Japan.
== Related Organizations == * Zentsuji Teaching Promotion Society. * Zentsuji Henshō Lecture Headquarters. * Zentsuji Henshō Society.
